facts.value: Alibaba co-led $1B Series B; became largest corporate stakeholder driving Kimi cloud integration
facts.excerpt: China's Moonshot AI zooms to $2.5B valuation, raising $1B for an LLM focused on long context
facts.notes: Equity investment, not a datacenter/power commitment; recorded as cloud_partnership because Alibaba is Moonshot's named cloud/compute provider. TechCrunch says Alibaba co-led the ~$1B round (with HongShan) at a $2.5B valuation. Separate reporting (Alibaba SEC 20-F / Wikipedia, en.wikipedia.org) puts Alibaba's stake at ~36% and investment at ~$0.8B USD; that specific stake figure was NOT in the TechCrunch page fetched, so treat as secondary/unverified. No MW, GPU count, or capex disclosed.

verification.verifier_note: The cited TechCrunch page supports the funding half only: it states Moonshot raised over $1B in Series B (at $2.5B valuation) and that 'Co-leading the round are e-commerce giant Alibaba and HongShan.' However, the page contains NO statement that Alibaba 'became the largest corporate stakeholder' or

facts.value: Alibaba Cloud provides compute (ACK/ECS/ACS, Ray/Spark) for Kimi data preprocessing and training
facts.excerpt: The solution is centered on Alibaba Cloud Container Service for Kubernetes (ACK), featuring deep optimizations for Ray and Spark tasks.
facts.notes: Moonshot RENTS this capacity from Alibaba Cloud; it is NOT Moonshot-owned. Source is the partner's own (Alibaba Cloud) engineering blog; describes ability to create 50,000+ pods in an ACK cluster and OSS/ECS/ACS usage for the Kimi model, but discloses NO GPU count, MW, or cluster size. English secondary/partner-marketing source; no independent verification of scale figures.

verification.verifier_note: Page (dated April 8, 2025) directly documents Alibaba Cloud providing compute infrastructure for Moonshot AI/Kimi data preprocessing and training. Explicitly cites ACK (Container Service for Kubernetes), ACS (Container Compute Service), OSS, and Ray+Spark frameworks for terabyte-scale data processin
